sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Brown & Brown (NYSE:BRO) Misses Q4 CY2025 Sales Expectations
feed.summary: Brown & Brown (NYSE:BRO) reported its Q4 CY2025 earnings, missing Wall Street's revenue expectations despite a 36% year-on-year sales increase to $1.61 billion. The company, however, beat non-GAAP profit estimates with $0.93 per share. While long-term revenue and EPS growth have been strong, the recent quarter saw a revenue miss, leading to a slight dip in stock price.

feed.title: Exxon begins commercial CCS project with CF industries in Louisiana
feed.summary: Exxon Mobil has initiated its commercial carbon capture and storage (CCS) project with ammonia producer CF Industries in Louisiana, beginning operation in 2025. This project aims to transport and store up to 2 million tonnes of carbon dioxide annually from CF Industries' Donaldsonville complex. Exxon also has agreements with other companies to manage CO2 from their planned projects and expects three additional CCS projects to come online in 2026, advancing its multiple CCS developments in Texas and Louisiana.

feed.title: Self-Storage REITs Schedule Call for 4Q 2025 Financial Results
feed.summary: The four largest publicly traded self-storage REITs—CubeSmart, Extra Space Storage Inc., National Storage Affiliates Trust, and Public Storage Inc.—have announced their schedules for releasing fourth-quarter 2025 financial results. Each company will host a conference call following their earnings release to discuss performance and answer questions from financial analysts. Investors can access webcasts and replays of these calls through the companies' respective investor-relations pages.

feed.title: STUBHUB ALERT: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. is Investigating
feed.summary: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. is investigating StubHub Holdings, Inc. on behalf of long-term stockholders following a class action complaint filed on November 24, 2025. The investigation focuses on whether StubHub's board of directors breached their fiduciary duties, specifically regarding allegations that the company's registration statement for its September 2025 IPO was materially false and misleading about payment timing changes and adverse impacts on free cash flow. The firm encourages affected investors to contact them to discuss their legal rights.

feed.title: Did Teledyne’s 2025 Records, Buyback and Space Wins Just Shift TDY’s Investment Narrative?
feed.summary: Teledyne Technologies reported strong full-year 2025 results, including record sales and net income, a share buyback, and critical space imaging deployments. The company's performance reinforces its position as a high-end sensor and imaging supplier, with steady, high-margin earnings, though it faces a premium valuation and dependency on defense budgets. These factors, alongside upcoming board transitions, shape Teledyne's evolving investment narrative.

feed.title: Up-and-coming Mexican chain dubbed 'the GOAT compared to Chipotle' to open another new spot in February
feed.summary: Pancheros Mexican Grill, a fast-casual chain with over 70 locations, is set to open a new spot in Springfield, Missouri, on February 2, 2026. This expansion brings the total number of Pancheros in Missouri to six, and the company is praised by some diners as superior to Chipotle due to its "Burritos Better Built" and in-house made tortillas. The article also notes the struggles of other Mexican chains facing closures or bankruptcy.

feed.title: Brown & Brown, Inc. Announces Fourth Quarter 2025 Results
feed.summary: Brown & Brown, Inc. announced its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2025. The company reported total revenues of $1.6 billion for Q4 2025, a 35.7% increase year-over-year, and $5.9 billion for the full year, up 22.8% from 2024. Diluted net income per share – adjusted increased to $0.93 for the quarter and $4.26 for the full year, demonstrating strong financial performance highlighted by strategic acquisitions and revenue growth.

feed.title: Stryker (SYK) Downgraded to Hold by TD Cowen
feed.summary: TD Cowen has downgraded Stryker (SYK) from "Buy" to "Hold" with a price target of $387, citing that the company's potential for exceeding earnings expectations is already factored into its current valuation. Stryker, a leading medical device company with a strong market position, exhibits robust financial health with consistent growth and profitability. Despite its solid performance, the premium valuation suggests a cautious approach for potential investors.

feed.title: Waste Management gives update for Pulaski County & Little Rock customers
feed.summary: Waste Management has updated its winter operations plans for Pulaski County and Little Rock customers due to ongoing winter weather impacts. Officials announced that services would not run on Tuesday, January 27, for residential recycling in Little Rock, North Little Rock, and Sherwood, as well as for trash and recycling in unincorporated areas of the county. Pickup will occur on the next scheduled day, with special instructions for overflowing recycling and non-recyclable waste.

feed.title: Revvity Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $0.07 a Share, Payable May 8 to Holders of Record April 17
feed.summary: Revvity Inc. has announced that it will maintain its quarterly dividend at $0.07 per share. This dividend is scheduled to be paid on May 8 to shareholders who are on record as of April 17. The biotechnology tools company specializes in diagnostic equipment and analytical instruments for various sectors.

feed.title: New $27 million permit issued for Smucker’s facility in McCalla
feed.summary: A $27 million building permit has been issued for a 4,150-square-foot addition to the J.M. Smucker Co. facility in McCalla, Alabama. This project, which produces Uncrustables, is part of a larger $1.1 billion commitment by Smucker’s in three phases, with The Dennis Group acting as the lead contractor. This permit was the largest of ten new construction permits exceeding $1 million issued in Alabama last week.

feed.title: Alexandria Real Estate Equities: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Alexandria Real Estate Equities Inc. (ARE) reported fourth-quarter funds from operations of $2.16 per share, exceeding Wall Street estimates of $2.15 per share. The company posted revenue of $754.4 million, also beating forecasts, despite an overall loss of $1.08 billion for the quarter. For the full year, the real estate investment trust reported funds from operations of $2.33 per share on $3.03 billion in revenue.

feed.title: Apple drops new AirTag. See what’s different.
feed.summary: Apple has announced a second-generation AirTag, featuring an upgraded second-generation Ultra Wideband chip for improved range and 50% higher volume. The new AirTag also includes enhanced privacy and security features and retains the original pricing of $29 for one or $99 for a four-pack. It is available for order now and will ship later this week.

feed.title: CF Industries and POET launch low-carbon fertilizer pilot project
feed.summary: CF Industries (NYSE:CF) and POET have launched a pilot project with agricultural cooperatives to create a low-carbon fertilizer supply chain, aiming to reduce the carbon intensity of corn production and lower the carbon footprint of ethanol. CF Industries, with its strong financial performance and nearly 20% year-to-date return, produces the low-carbon ammonia at its Donaldsonville Complex by capturing and storing CO2 emissions. This initiative aligns with the company's financial strength and robust free cash flow, with Wells Fargo maintaining an "Overweight" rating on CF Industries due to its positive nitrogen outlook and industry-leading free cash flow.

feed.title: Brown & Brown: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Brown & Brown Inc. reported a fourth-quarter profit of $264 million, or 93 cents per share adjusted, surpassing Wall Street's expectations of 91 cents per share. However, the insurance company's revenue of $1.61 billion missed analyst forecasts. For the full year, the company posted a profit of $1.05 billion on revenue of $5.9 billion.

feed.title: ROSS STORES ANNOUNCES THE PASSING OF FORMER CHAIRMAN AND CEO NORMAN A. FERBER
feed.summary: Ross Stores, Inc. announced the passing of Norman Ferber, former Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, at the age of 77. Mr. Ferber was instrumental in the company's growth from a small chain to a leading off-price retailer and remained deeply involved for almost 30 years after his CEO tenure. The company has honored his legacy with contributions to the Ross Cares Fund and a donation to the Boys & Girls Clubs of America, where he was a dedicated volunteer and donor.

feed.title: From 6 stores to a $21B chain: Ross architect Norman Ferber dies at 77
feed.summary: Norman A. Ferber, former Chairman and CEO of Ross Stores, has passed away at the age of 77. He was a pivotal figure in growing Ross from a small six-store chain into a major off-price retailer, serving in various leadership roles and continuing as an advisor for nearly 30 years. Ross Stores is honoring his legacy with contributions to the Ross Cares Fund and the Ross Stores Foundation, and a room at the Moldaw-Zaffaroni Clubhouse was named in his honor.

feed.title: ROSS STORES ANNOUNCES THE PASSING OF FORMER CHAIRMAN AND CEO NORMAN A. FERBER
feed.summary: Ross Stores, Inc. announced the passing of Norman Ferber, former Chairman and CEO, at the age of 77. Ferber was instrumental in the company's growth from a small chain to a leading off-price retailer, serving in various leadership roles and providing counsel for decades. The company honored his legacy with contributions to the Ross Cares Fund and the Boys & Girls Clubs of America, where he was a dedicated volunteer.

feed.title: Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOGL) Trading Up 1.6% After Analyst Upgrade
feed.summary: Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOGL) shares rose 1.6% after Wolfe Research increased its price target to $390.00. The stock continues to hold a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts, though significant insider selling has been noted recently, raising some near-term concerns. Major institutional investors have also shown considerable activity, with Norges Bank and Berkshire Hathaway Inc. acquiring substantial new stakes.

feed.title: Conagra Brands Target of Unusually Large Options Trading (NYSE:CAG)
feed.summary: Conagra Brands (NYSE:CAG) experienced unusually large options trading activity, with investors purchasing 99,242 call options, a 905% increase from average. Despite a recent earnings beat and a dividend announcement, analyst sentiment is weak with an average "Reduce" rating and a consensus price target of $18.71, while the stock trades around $18.09. Insider selling and a negative P/E ratio are also noted.

feed.title: Did BlackRock Build A New Floor for Archer's Stock Price?
feed.summary: BlackRock Inc. has increased its stake in Archer Aviation to 8.1%, signaling a vote of confidence in the electric aviation company. This institutional backing, combined with Archer's strong financial position and strategic partnership with Stellantis, positions the company favorably in the eVTOL sector. The move also creates potential for a short squeeze due to high short interest and institutional accumulation, contrasting with competitor Joby Aviation's recent valuation headwinds.

feed.title: $27 million project at Smucker’s plant among new building permits
feed.summary: A $27 million building permit has been approved for a 4,150-square-foot expansion at the J.M. Smucker Co. plant in McCalla, Alabama. This project is part of Smucker's larger $1.1 billion commitment to the facility, which began operations in November 2024 producing Uncrustables. The new permit is one of ten statewide exceeding $1 million, with many located in North Alabama.

feed.title: Medtronic Sphere-360 PFA catheter lands CE mark
feed.summary: Medtronic's Sphere-360, a new PFA catheter, has received CE mark approval and has begun its investigational device exemption HORIZON trial in the U.S. This is the second catheter in Medtronic's Affera PFA franchise, designed to treat paroxysmal atrial fibrillation. Atrial fibrillation affects millions globally and this device aims to address the intermittent erratic heartbeat associated with the condition.

feed.title: Dominion Energy crews still working to restore power in northeastern counties
feed.summary: Dominion Energy crews are actively working to restore power in Bertie County due to lingering effects of a winter storm. This comes as other energy providers like Edgecombe EMC have largely managed outages, with only one remaining reported. The wider region is still experiencing the impact of the storm, affecting power for many residents.

feed.title: After-hours movers: Humana, UnitedHealth, CVS Health, Sanmina and more
feed.summary: Shares of UnitedHealth Group, Humana, CVS Health, Alignment Healthcare, and Elevance Health fell significantly in after-hours trading following a Trump administration proposal for lower-than-expected Medicare Advantage payment rates for 2027. Sanmina also saw a drop after releasing disappointing Q2 2026 revenue guidance, while Nucor fell after missing consensus estimates on its quarterly results. The proposed 0.09% average increase in Medicare Advantage payments by CMS negatively impacted health insurers.

feed.title: Brown & Brown shares drop after revenue miss in fourth quarter
feed.summary: Brown & Brown, Inc. (NYSE:BRO) saw its shares drop by 3.7% in after-hours trading despite beating adjusted earnings per share expectations in the fourth quarter. The decline was attributed to a revenue miss, with total revenue of $1.6 billion falling short of the $1.65 billion consensus forecast, and a decrease in organic revenue by 2.8% for the quarter. For the full year 2025, the insurance brokerage firm reported robust total revenue growth and improved adjusted EBITDAC margins.

feed.title: Hologic Faces Merger-Related Stockholder Suits, Issues Disclosures
feed.summary: Hologic is facing stockholder lawsuits regarding disclosures for its proposed merger with Hopper Parent Inc., leading to the issuance of supplemental proxy information. The company is also dealing with an extended recall of Brevera 9 Gauge Needles, which impacts its Breast Health revenue and contingent value rights milestones. Additionally, a BioZorb product liability litigation has largely been settled without financial liability for Hologic through an insurance-covered agreement.

feed.title: Teradyne (TER) Maintains Quarterly Dividend of $0.12 Per Share
feed.summary: Teradyne (TER) has announced it will maintain its quarterly dividend of $0.12 per share, reflecting stability in shareholder returns with a forward yield of 0.21%. The company, a key player in semiconductor testing and industrial automation, despite facing revenue growth challenges, demonstrates strong financial health with robust margins, low debt levels, and a high Altman Z-Score. Its valuation metrics suggest a premium trading position, and institutional ownership indicates strong market interest.

feed.title: Nucor (NUE) Reports Lower Than Expected Q4 Revenue
feed.summary: Nucor Corporation (NUE) reported fourth-quarter revenue of $7.69 billion, falling short of analyst expectations. Despite missing revenue targets, the company highlights significant advancements in infrastructure projects and expresses optimism for 2026 due to strong market demand and supportive federal policies. However, investors are cautioned about the stock's current high valuation and potential market volatility.

feed.title: Blackstone settles with Department of Justice in RealPage lawsuit
feed.summary: Blackstone's LivCor subsidiary has settled with the Department of Justice regarding allegations of using an anticompetitive pricing algorithm from RealPage. This settlement prevents Blackstone from using such algorithms, sharing sensitive data with competitors, and requires a court-appointed monitor if using uncertified third-party pricing software. The agreement is part of a broader series of settlements related to RealPage, a property management software accused of facilitating price collusion and inflating rental prices across the housing market.

feed.title: Humana Launches $3 Million Scholarship Fund For Xavier Ochsner College Of Medicine
feed.summary: Humana has established a $3 million scholarship fund for medical students at the Xavier Ochsner College of Medicine (XOCOM) to address physician shortages and improve healthcare access in Louisiana. The Humana Health Workforce Scholarship Fund will provide full-tuition scholarships, prioritizing students with financial need and a commitment to primary care, internal medicine, or maternal care in the state post-graduation. This initiative builds on Humana's existing relationship with Xavier University of Louisiana and aims to increase the number of physicians trained to deliver integrated care.

feed.title: Low-carbon fertilizer test targets 5–6M gallons of cleaner ethanol
feed.summary: CF Industries and POET have launched a pilot project with major agriculture co-operatives, including WinField United, NuWay-K&H, New Cooperative, and Farmer’s Cooperative, to create a low-carbon fertilizer supply chain. This initiative aims to reduce the carbon intensity of corn used for ethanol production, with POET expecting to produce 5-6 million gallons of lower carbon intensity ethanol. The collaboration demonstrates a viable pathway for decarbonizing bioethanol inputs, linking low-carbon fertilizers from production to ethanol output.

feed.title: Pinnacle Financial Partners Announces Common Dividend
feed.summary: Pinnacle Financial Partners has announced a cash dividend of $0.50 per share for common shareholders, payable on February 27, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 6, 2026. The firm, a $119.1 billion asset regional bank, recently joined forces with Synovus Financial Corp. in 2026. Pinnacle is recognized as a top employer in financial services and as the largest bank headquartered in Tennessee.

feed.title: ONEOK appoints McCollum and Owodunni to board of directors
feed.summary: ONEOK, a midstream energy company, has appointed Mark A. McCollum and Precious Williams Owodunni as independent directors to its board, effective January 23, 2026. McCollum brings extensive energy industry and financial experience, while Owodunni contributes expertise in talent retention, business strategy, finance, and corporate governance. The company maintains a strong financial position with consistent dividend payments and is currently considered undervalued by InvestingPro.

feed.title: Alamo Group completes acquisition of Petersen Industries
feed.summary: Alamo Group Inc. has completed its acquisition of Petersen Industries, a manufacturer of specialized truck-mounted grapple loader equipment, enhancing Alamo's industrial and vegetation management portfolio. The acquisition supports Alamo's strategic growth initiatives and will leverage its distribution network to strengthen Petersen's market position. Alamo Group, which holds more cash than debt, plans to focus on customer satisfaction and product innovation in this new venture.

feed.title: Copart secures $1.25 billion unsecured credit facility maturing in 2031
feed.summary: Copart Inc. (NASDAQ:CPRT) has secured a new $1.25 billion unsecured senior revolving credit agreement maturing in 2031, replacing a previous facility. This agreement, with Wells Fargo Bank as administrative agent, includes options for extensions and allows for borrowings in various currencies and substantial sub-facilities for its international subsidiaries. The company plans to use the proceeds for general corporate purposes, including working capital, capital expenditures, dividends, share repurchases, and acquisitions.

feed.title: Adeia appoints new executives to strengthen growth strategy
feed.summary: Adeia Inc. (NASDAQ:ADEA) has announced key executive appointments to bolster its long-term growth strategy. Craig Mitchell has rejoined as chief semiconductor officer, while Dr. Mark Kokes and Bill Thomas have been named chief revenue officer and chief strategy officer, respectively. These changes follow Adeia's recent Q3 2025 earnings report, which, despite missing expectations, was accompanied by raised 2025 revenue guidance due to a significant licensing agreement with Adobe Inc. and a subsequent stock price target increase by Rosenblatt.

feed.title: PPG named sole supplier of coatings for Quality Collision Group body shops
feed.summary: PPG (NYSE: PPG) has been named the sole supplier of automotive refinish coatings for Quality Collision Group's (QCG) over 95 collision repair centers across 13 states. This partnership will provide QCG with PPG's premium coatings, advanced digital tools, technical expertise, and specialized training to boost productivity and efficiency. Both companies emphasize innovation, performance, and sustainable growth within the automotive refinish industry.

feed.title: Traeger (NYSE: COOK) asks investors to back reverse split to address NYSE price rule
feed.summary: Traeger, Inc. (NYSE: COOK) is seeking investor approval for a reverse stock split at a ratio between 1-for-10 and 1-for-50. The primary goal is to increase its share price to regain compliance with the NYSE's minimum $1.00 bid price requirement, following a notice received on November 19, 2025. Stockholders will vote on this proposal at a virtual special meeting on March 2, 2026, with the board retaining discretion on whether and when to implement the split.

feed.title: Alamo Group Completes Acquisition Of Petersen Industries
feed.summary: Alamo Group has announced the completion of its acquisition of Petersen Industries. This merger and acquisition news was reported by Reuters and involves Alamo Group (ALG). The article is a brief statement on the finalized deal.

feed.title: Sanmina: Fiscal Q1 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Sanmina Corp. (SANM) reported an adjusted profit of $2.38 per share on revenue of $3.19 billion for its fiscal first quarter. The company announced net income of $49.3 million, or 89 cents per share. Looking ahead, Sanmina forecasts fiscal second-quarter earnings between $2.25 and $2.55 per share, with revenue projected to be between $3.1 billion and $3.4 billion.

feed.title: Palo Alto Networks stock climbs after the bell as Fed decision nears — what traders watch next
feed.summary: Palo Alto Networks stock rose 2.2% after hours, alongside other cybersecurity firms, ahead of the Federal Reserve's rate decision on Wednesday. Investors are also anticipating major tech earnings reports, though challenges remain for cybersecurity companies regarding customer spending and risks associated with large acquisitions. The Fed's policy statement and Chair Jerome Powell's remarks are the next critical events.

feed.title: Susquehanna sees CSX refocusing on fundamentals under new CEO
feed.summary: Susquehanna analysts believe that CSX will refocus on business fundamentals and improve operational metrics under its new CEO, Joseph Hinrichs. The firm upgraded CSX shares to "positive" from "neutral," citing confidence in Hinrichs' ability to implement a more collaborative and communicative approach, which is expected to support stable growth and potentially result in a re-rating of the stock.

feed.title: Alexandria Real Estate Equities: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Alexandria Real Estate Equities Inc. (ARE) reported its fourth-quarter earnings, with key profitability measures surpassing Wall Street expectations. The real estate investment trust posted funds from operations of $368.5 million, or $2.16 per share, exceeding analyst estimates of $2.15 per share. Despite reporting a loss of $1.08 billion, the company's revenue of $754.4 million also beat forecasts, and its shares have increased 17% since the beginning of the year.

feed.title: Copart Signs Senior Revolving Credit Agreement With Wells Fargo
feed.summary: Copart has secured a new $1.25 billion senior revolving credit facility with Wells Fargo, which includes a $500 million incremental option and matures in 2031. This new agreement replaces their previous credit agreement with Bank of America, providing enhanced liquidity for working capital, capital expenditures, dividends, buybacks, and domestic and international expansion. The unsecured facility aims to increase Copart's financial flexibility and support its growth initiatives.

feed.title: Ten Holdings to Present at DealFlow Discovery Conference
feed.summary: Ten Holdings, Inc. (XHLD) announced its plan to present at the DealFlow Discovery Conference on January 28-29, 2026, in Atlantic City, New Jersey. The company released an updated investor presentation to engage more effectively with investors and communicate its strategy. Despite this, TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates XHLD as Neutral due to weak financial performance and bearish technical conditions, although recent corporate actions offer some support.

feed.title: Priority Income Fund to redeem half of Series J preferred shares
feed.summary: Priority Income Fund, Inc. announced its decision to redeem 50% of its outstanding 6.000% Series J Term Preferred Stock Due 2028. This move involves redeeming 790,000 shares at $25 each, plus accrued dividends, with the redemption date set for February 25, 2026. After this, the fund will retain three other series of preferred stock, continuing its focus on senior secured loans and collateralized loan obligations, and is managed by Priority Senior Secured Income Management, LLC.

feed.title: Revvity declares quarterly dividend of $0.07 per share
feed.summary: Revvity, Inc. announced a regular quarterly dividend of $0.07 per share, payable on May 8, 2026, to shareholders of record as of April 17, 2026. This dividend marks the company's 56th consecutive year of payments, reflecting a current yield of 0.24%. Despite trading at a premium valuation with a market cap of $13.02 billion and a P/E ratio of 59.38, the health science solutions provider is set to report its next quarterly earnings in three days.

feed.title: Nucor shares drop as fourth quarter earnings miss estimates
feed.summary: Nucor Corp (NYSE:NUE) reported fourth-quarter earnings and revenue that missed analyst expectations, leading to a 3.2% drop in its shares during after-hours trading. Despite the miss, the company's adjusted quarterly earnings improved significantly year-over-year. Nucor anticipates an increase in earnings across all operating segments in the first quarter of 2026 and maintained a strong financial position with substantial cash reserves.

feed.title: UnitedHealth, Humana, CVS Shares Get Crushed As Trump Administration Proposes Lower-Than-Expected Medicare Insurer Rates
feed.summary: Shares of major health insurers UnitedHealth Group, Humana, and CVS Health dropped significantly after hours following reports that the Trump administration plans to propose "roughly flat" Medicare insurer rates for 2027, an increase of only 0.09%. This proposal is much lower than the 4% to 6% increase analysts had anticipated, causing concern across the industry. The Medicare agency is also expected to target billing practices that have historically been very profitable for insurers.
